chief_complaint: An 86-year-old man presents with non-fluent aphasia of the mixed type and acquired apraxia of speech after an ischemic stroke in the left hemisphere.
history: The patient was diagnosed with an ischemic stroke in the left hemisphere, resulting in non-fluent aphasia of the mixed type, accompanied by acquired apraxia of speech. He has four years of formal education.
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination: not provided.
labs: ECG: not provided. Bloods: not provided. Imaging: ischemic stroke in the left hemisphere. Montreal-Toulouse Language Assessment Battery - Brief Version and Naming and Visual Recognition Test results: improvements noted in word naming and verbal word comprehension, reduction in linguistic errors such as paraphasias.
correct_diagnosis: Non-fluent aphasia of the mixed type with acquired apraxia of speech after ischemic stroke in the left hemisphere
differentials: Non-fluent aphasia of the mixed type with acquired apraxia of speech after ischemic stroke in the left hemisphere
Anomic aphasia after ischemic stroke in the right hemisphere
Global aphasia after ischemic stroke in the left hemisphere
Transcortical motor aphasia after ischemic stroke in the left hemisphere
correct_ranking: Non-fluent aphasia of the mixed type with acquired apraxia of speech after ischemic stroke in the left hemisphere
Global aphasia after ischemic stroke in the left hemisphere
Transcortical motor aphasia after ischemic stroke in the left hemisphere
Anomic aphasia after ischemic stroke in the right hemisphere
